mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 in rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5d returns during tightening periods, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation readings above the 315 threshold indicate persistent price pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 315.0 correlates with positive 20d returns in > 55% of observations over a 2-year sample, invalidating the compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60d returns in > 50% of cases, breaking the labor-demand transmission.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly translates to stronger corporate earnings fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in improved economic output.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows zero or negative correlation with 20d forward returns across all tested rolling windows, rejecting the earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production combined with elevated inflation creates stagflationary pressures that erode corporate margins and trigger aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined threshold condition fails to predict negative 5d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.
